sections.paragraph: ξενία, ας, ἡ [ξένος; ‘entertainment, hospitality’] in Ac 28:23 ξ. may refer in the usual sense of the word to hospitality, as extended by Paul to his visitors; the same mng. may apply in Phlm 22, except that it is Paul who looks forward to hospitality. Whether in both cases ξ. is to be understood by metonymy as guest room or simply lodging cannot be determined with certainty.
